description_long: This is a monumental stone sculpture featuring a regal lion lying in a protective posture at the base of a large, inscribed pedestal. The lion is highly detailed, with a flowing mane and muscular body, exuding strength and dignity. Draped fabric is sculpted behind the lion, adding a sense of movement and realism to the composition. The pedestal behind the lion bears an inscription dedicated to Queen Victoria, identifying her as 'Queen and Empress, a model wife and mother.' The structure is robust, with a broad base and a partially damaged or weathered upper section. The overall appearance suggests a commemorative or memorial function, likely situated in a public or ceremonial space.
description: A detailed stone sculpture of a lion resting in front of an inscribed pedestal dedicated to Queen Victoria, with draped fabric and a robust, weathered base.
description_view_0: The front view shows the lion in full detail, lying in front of the inscribed pedestal with the dedication to Queen Victoria clearly visible.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the lion's profile and the depth of the pedestal, with less visibility of the inscription and more focus on the lion's mane and body.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the back of the lion and the draped fabric, with the inscription on the pedestal visible in reverse and less detail on the lion's face.
description_view_3: The right side view displays the lion's back and tail, as well as the side of the pedestal, with minimal visibility of the inscription.
attribute_subject_matter: lion and commemorative pedestal dedicated to Queen Victoria
attribute_location_context: The sculpture appears to be installed in an outdoor public or ceremonial setting, likely in a park or plaza, as suggested by the monumental scale and commemorative inscription.
